1.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1.13.1. Create a new Inventory Committee composed of an adequate number of
members to complete the physical inventory in three months or less with
at least one member each from the accounting and property
divisions/unit;
1.13.2. Instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan
(PIP) with specified duties and assignments of the members and submit
the PIP to the audit team before the scheduled inventory-taking
activities;
1.13.3. Temporarily relieve the members of the Inventory Committee of all their
regular duties so they can fully devote their time to conducting the
physical inventory until its completion;
1.13.4. Request for a COA representative to witness the inventory-taking; and
1.13.5. Direct the Inventory Committee to strictly follow the general and
procedural guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020 in the conduct of the physical count of PPE and the one-
time cleansing of PPE account balances.

2.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to ensure proper
inventory management and accurate financial reporting by requiring the
following actions:
2.11.1. The Municipal Treasurer shall conduct a physical count of inventories
every semester and submit a corresponding report to the Municipal
Accountant and the Auditor no later than July 31 for the first semester
and January 31 for the second semester.
2.11.2. The Municipal Treasurer shall submit the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant on a weekly
basis, duly supported by Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS), to serve as
the basis for recording utilized inventory items in the books.
2.11.3. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall
regularly reconcile accounting and inventory records and implement
the necessary adjustments to ensure the fair presentation of accounts
in the financial statements.

3.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
3.12.1. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the
difference between the RPT and SET Receivables account balances
totaling ₱8,907,556.24 and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation,
together with the Municipal Assessor, of the total collectibles from the
Basic and Special Education Tax with the RPT and SET Receivables
account balances to prevent the accumulation of unreconciled
differences;
3.12.2. The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly
certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the
current year as basis for recording the RPT and SET Receivable
accounts in compliance with Chapter 3, Section 20 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume I; and
3.12.3. The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for RPT and
SET receivables to support the general account in the financial
statements.

4.9. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant, with the concurrence of the Local Chief Executive,
evaluate the collectability of these loans and exhaust all efforts to collect from
the debtors, such as sending demand letters, conducting follow-ups, negotiating
payment terms, or implementing legal actions.

4.10. Henceforth, we recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant maintain subsidiary records for future borrowers to monitor each
borrower’s status and provide detailed records that support the General Ledger
to ensure accurate financial reporting.

5.11. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct:
5.11.1. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers
(DVs) and claims against public funds are supported with complete,
accurate, and relevant documents in accordance with Section 4(6) of
Presidential Decree No. 1445, which states that claims must be backed by
complete documentation.
5.11.2. The Municipal Treasurer to mandate the use of “PAID” stamps on all
DVs and supporting documents immediately after payment to establish
proof of settlement, prevent duplicate disbursements, and maintain a
clear audit trail.
5.11.3. The LDRRM Officer to review the Activity Design of all LDRRM
activities to ensure that the planned objectives, target participants, and
budget allocations are clearly defined, realistic, and aligned with actual
implementation.
5.11.4. All Department Heads to use the updated forms such as the DV, PR etc.
as prescribed in the Manual of New Government Accounting System for
LGUs.
5.11.5. All Department Heads and personnel responsible for any transaction to
review, sign, and fill in all the information needed in the Disbursement
Voucher, Purchase Request, Requests for Quotation, Purchase Orders,
Activity Designs, Acceptance and Inspection Reports, and other related
pertinent documents.

60. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to establish and maintain a GAD database in order to provide the data necessary to properly plan GAD projects and activities.
61. We further recommended that the municipal mayor create a gender-responsive Monitoring and Evaluation System in order that GAD projects and activities are properly planned and implemented and its results assessed to ensure its success.
observations.managementAction:
CBMS started last October 2022 but not completed.
No action had been done to create the GAD-responsive M&E System.

observations.recommendation:
62. We recommended that Program of Work be properly prepared based on detailed engineering measurements, duly reviewed by the Municipal Engineer before the same is approved in order to avoid the purchase of excess materials and loss of government funds, and that in cases where excesses cannot be avoided, the Municipal Engineer see to it that the excess materials are reprogrammed for utilization in other projects of the Municipality.
63. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit an explanation for the excess quantities of materials programmed, the neglect to properly account and store the excess and to reprogram these to other projects of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Programs of Work had not been attached to the disbursement vouchers.
The Municipal Engineer had not submitted a formal explanation on the excessive quantities of materials.
observations.reason: Workload and other priorities; POWs had not been attached to the DVs.

observations.recommendation:
64. We recommended that the provincial treasurer of the Liga Ng Mga Barangay of Negros Oriental, to acknowledge its payment with government accountable form No. 51 in order that the funds may be subject to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
65. We also recommended the provincial liga adhere to DILG MC 2011-59.
observations.managementAction:
A letter request had already been sent to the concerned personnel of the Liga but no answer had been given.
The Municipality cannot compel the provincial liga.

observations.recommendation: 66. We recommended that in the preparation of the 20% Development Fund Budget, a Program of Work be required before a project can be included therein in order that the implementation thereof will not be delayed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer found it impractical to prepare the Programs of Work in the budgeting process due to price escalations/increase. The Municipality had other backlog projects that they prioritized.
observations.reason: Due to price escalations/increase. Backlog projects were prioritized.

observations.recommendation: 68. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the municipal government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the Municipality of Ayungon can be issued to establish the Municipality's ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: Time and work load constraints prevented the implementation of the recommendation (reiterated).

observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a subsidiary ledger and
reconcile their record with the Municipal Treasurer to record an accurate account balance
in the financial statement.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor determine the value of
the five properties and maintain a complete record and documentation of the real
properties owned by the Municipality.

1.11. The discrepancies in land items totaling ₱818,863.54 may now be recorded at
the same cost. Additionally, we recommend adjusting the land account to reflect the
market value, increasing it by ₱6,577,000.00 to rectify the understatement. It is
important to maintain and regularly update subsidiary ledgers to prevent similar errors in
the future.

2.10. We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to prioritize the
filing of applications for the titling of all real properties acquired by the municipality so
that TCTs can be issued in favor of the Municipality of Ayungon to establish ownership
thereto.

3.9. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce the collection of
delinquent real property taxes by availing of the remedies for collecting delinquent real
property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160, which are administrative
action through levy on real property or judicial action.

4.6. We recommended that the concerned end-user submit to the audit team
documentation of the cost components considered to arrive at the ABC for the
procurement of the brand-new backhoe and LED wall. In addition, we recommended that
the BAC Chairman conduct a thorough review of the ABC for all procurements before it
is submitted for approval to the Municipal Mayor to ensure transparency and
accountability of the procurement process.

5.11. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure strict adherence to
existing laws and regulations and that all accountable officers and employees under his
jurisdiction be properly bonded to provide effective bond coverage in case of shortages
or losses in compliance with Section 101 of PD No. 1445.

5.12. Furthermore, we recommended that the Revenue Collection Clerks assume
their duties and responsibilities as collectors of the LGU and stop the practice of
allowing job orders or casual employees to collect on their behalf.

6.7. We recommended and Management agreed to submit the lacking documents and,
henceforth, ensure that the supporting documents are complete in accordance with
Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

6. We recommended and Management agreed that they require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS within 10 days from receipt of the bank statements as required under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 so that the validity and existence of the Cash in Bank account could be ascertained.
7. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the balances of Cash in Bank in the BRS reconcile with that in the FS.
observations.managementAction:
BRS had been complied by the Municipal Accountant since June 2023.
The Municipal Accountant ensured that the balances of Cash in Bank in the BRS reconciled with those in the FS.

observations.recommendation:
22. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to fast-track the preparation of the financial statements and submit them to our office at the soonest possible time.
23. We further recommended that Management closely monitor the strict adherence to the requirements of pertinent laws and regulation relating to the submission of financial statements and other accounting reports, and cause the suspension of payment of salaries of employees responsible for the delayed submission or non-submission until the same shall be complied.
observations.managementAction:
The CY 2023 financial statements have been submitted on February 14, 2024.
The Municipal Accountant had exerted all efforts to submit the financial statements on time.

observations.recommendation:
33. We recommended that Management provide a written explanation regarding the payment of the purchase of rice through cash advances, and refrain from granting cash advance for payment of regular expenditures of the Municipality in the future.
34. Moreover, the rules and regulations on the nature granting and utilization of cash advance be strictly followed in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction:
A written justification had already been submitted.
No cash advances of this nature had been granted since issuance of this audit observation and subsequent recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
45. We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification to prove reasonableness for the procurement of the aforecited items above the suggested price of the DOH.
46. Henceforth, procure medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the SRP set by appropriate government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction:
The MHO had already submitted a written justification.
The medical supplies were procured through public bidding.

observations.recommendation: 52. We recommended that Management instruct the LDRRM Officer to include PPAs chargeable against the prior years unexpended LDRRMF in the annual LDRRMFIP of the current year pursuant to NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-1 and the same be prepared in substantial adherence to the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002, thereby make this trust fund available for disbursement.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer had been instructed to include PPAs chargeable against the prior years unexpended LDRRMF in the annual LDRRMFIP and the same be prepared in substantial adherence to the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: 53. We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to identify the amount of the LDRRM Fund related to CO which was transferred to the Special Trust Fund, and transfer it back to the unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund – CO to the General Fund to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements, and that the funds will not be made available for other disaster risk reduction and management activities.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2023, no CO had been transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: 54. We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and Management prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit the report to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF had been submitted promptly and regularly.

observations.recommendation: 67. We recommended that Management give priority to the implementation of GAD projects especially those which were not implemented in CY 2016 in order that the needs of women and the objectives of the program will be achieved.
observations.managementAction:
Implementation rate = 72%
Priority had been given to GAD projects in subsequent years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the SB member to acknowledge its payment with government accountable form No. 51 in order that the funds may be subject to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations. We also recommended that the provincial liga adhere to DILG MC 2011-59.
observations.managementAction: A letter request had already been sent to the provincial treasurer of the Liga and a verbal answer was made stating that she was unaware of the whereabouts.
observations.reason: The provincial Liga had not provided the official receipt; the whereabouts of the funds were still unresolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of the 20% Development Fund Budget, a Program of Work be required before a project can be included therein in order that the implementation under the 20% DF will not be delayed.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO indicated that it was impractical to prepare the Programs of Work in the budgeting process due to price escalations. The Municipality had other backlog projects that they prioritized.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer found it impractical to prepare the Programs of Work in the budgeting process due to price escalations and other backlog priorities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the municipal government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the Municipality of Ayungon can be issued to establish the Municipality's ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor indicated that the titling had not been prioritized because such was not part of her official duties. No formal action had been taken to expedite titling.
observations.reason: The application for titling had not been prioritized because the Municipal Assessor indicated it was not part of her official duties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer dispose obsolete and unserviceable properties and the Municipal Accountant to record the disposal in accordance with Sections 162 and 195 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 10, 1992.
observations.managementAction: There was no disposal since the physical count was not completed. No basis for recording of disposal was available.
observations.reason: There was no time to implement and the physical count was not completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust the
balances related to the PPE account as a change in accounting policy and,
subsequently, record depreciation based on a 5% residual value in compliance
with PAG 6 of IPSAS No. 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment. We also
recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that subsidiary ledgers be
maintained for all PPE accounts to ensure the accuracy and reliability of their
recorded book value.

We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor the following
documents to substantiate the grant of Hazard Pay:

a) Approved DTRs;
b) Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious
Diseases Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace; and the
c) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the period of
the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well as the entitlement of
the COVID-19 Hazard Pay.

We recommended and Management agreed that the irregular procedure
in the grant of cash advances, such as the grant of multiple cash advances to the
DO – Designate in one day be immediately stopped.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate
additional DO to avoid granting multiple cash advances to the incumbent DO.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of granting
honoraria to BAC observers. We further recommended that the honoraria
granted to BAC Observers be refunded by the claimants thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to fast-
track the preparation of the financial statements and submit them to our office
at the soonest possible time.

We further recommended that Management closely monitor the strict
adherence to the requirements of pertinent laws and regulation relating to the
submission of financial statements and other accounting reports, and cause the
suspension of payment of salaries of employees responsible for the delayed
submission or non-submission until the same shall be complied.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all
department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished
AAPSI and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the
Auditor at the soonest possible time, and within 60 days upon receipt of the
AARs for the succeeding years.

We further recommended and Management agreed that pertinent
documents to support the Actions Taken should also be submitted to facilitate
our validation of the responses.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. direct the Treasurer and the Accountant to coordinate with GSIS,
Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to determine the outstanding premium
contributions of each employee in their records;
b. ensure that mandatory contributions are remitted in accordance with
the provisions of R.A. No. 8291, R.A. No. 9679, and R.A. No. 7875 as
amended by R.A. No. 9241 and R.A. No. 10606 and its IRRs to avoid
the incurrence of interest and penalties; and
c. provide justification for the non-remittance. Any additional interest
charges and penalties that may be imposed for late remittances shall
be shouldered by the responsible officers.

We recommended that: a. Management direct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the physical count of inventories and properties and that RPCI and RPCPPE be submitted semestrally and annually, respectively; b. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be required to maintain and subsequently update the Ledger Cards and Stock and Property Cards in order that the reconciliation of the physical count against book balances can be facilitated; and c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the accounting and property records and make the necessary adjustments so that the accounts can be fairly presented in the financial statements.
In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, specifically charging of inventory to their appropriate expense accounts upon issuance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The physical count had been initiated 2014 2013 2012 2011 but was not completed. Management had not submitted any report. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer charged inventory to their appropriate expense accounts at the end of the year and not upon issuance thereof.
observations.reason: The physical count had not been completed due to time constraint and heavy workloads. Management had not submitted any report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management desist from charging ineligible programs, projects, and expenditures from BGCM and refund the BGCM for the amount expended to be sourced out from the General Fund Proper.
observations.managementAction: Fuel expenses for CY 2021 were for COVID-19-related programs, projects, and activities only. Furthermore, the ₱197,335.00 was refunded from the General Fund – Proper.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete documents in order to ascertain the propriety of the disbursement made; b. Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with completely filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption; c. Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to completely fill out the necessary data or information on the driver’s trip ticket; and d. Ensure that meals, snacks, catering services are duly supported with activity design, menu, and attendance of barangay officials and volunteers involved in the activity.
observations.managementAction: Trip tickets had already been filled out with the necessary data. The passengers’ documents had been submitted. Trip tickets had been filled out but some lacked the passengers’ signatures. Purchase orders had the necessary data. Management had not submitted the activity design, menu, and attendance of barangay officials.
observations.reason: The supporting documents had not been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide a written explanation regarding the payment of the purchase of rice through cash purchase of rice advances, and refrain from granting of cash advance for payment of regular expenditures of the Municipality in the future. Moreover, the rules and regulations on the granting and utilization of cash advance be strictly followed in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: A written justification had already been made. Management had planned to refrain from granting cash advance for payment of regular expenditures of the Municipality in the future. The DO assured the Audit Team that she would account for and liquidate all her cash advances at the end of every month. Management also commented that there is no need to appoint or designate an additional DO since the Municipality had planned to make salary payments through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) cards to lessen the cash advances granted to the DO.
observations.reason: Management still allowed the cash advances for the DO and multiple cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. verify that projects to be funded by the 20% DF, and disbursements made thereof conform to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in order that the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes mainly towards the creation of jobs, promotion of economic growth and eradication of poverty, as envisioned by the national government, may be achieved; b. immediately stop from utilizing the 20% DF for projects, programs and activities which are not properly chargeable thereto; c. the amount representing portion of the 20% DF spent for expenses which are not properly chargeable thereto, should be refunded from the appropriations of the General Fund Proper; and d. advise the MPDC Officer to prepare a revised AIP for the 20% DF for the realignment of those unobligated appropriations which were not properly chargeable thereto.
observations.managementAction: A refund to the 20% DF had already been made. The MPDC prepared the revised AIP and realigned appropriations as necessary. Some maintenance and other operating expenses (MOOEs) programmed under 20% DF remained.
observations.reason: There were still maintenance and other operating expenses (MOOEs) programmed under the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management direct the Municipal Accountant to furnish the Auditor copies of the contract of the foregoing projects together with the supporting documents for review of the latter. Moreover, Management is required to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The contracts and supporting documents for the infrastructure projects had already been submitted. Management adhered to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: The contracts had not been submitted within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (a) the MSWDO submit a written explanation on the difference noted between the number of the target beneficiaries (8,047) and the actual beneficiaries found on the master list (8,031) and on the lapses and deficiencies enumerated in this observation; (b) require Management to establish a system in the distribution of similar cash assistance programs wherein the guidelines issued by the DSWD are followed and that proper validation and appropriate profiling of beneficiaries are performed before disbursement; and (c) the amount received by the 13 job order personnel of the LGU who appeared in the master list of paid beneficiaries of the ESP be refunded.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted an explanation and initiated measures to improve inventory control and distribution monitoring. The Municipal Accountant charged the purchased goods to their appropriate expense accounts at year-end and not upon issuance thereof.
observations.reason: The MSWDO had not submitted additional documents. Some actions remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) establish a system in the distribution of similar cash assistance programs wherein the guidelines issued by the DSWD are followed and that proper validation and appropriate profiling of beneficiaries are performed before disbursement; and (b) the amount received by the 13 job order personnel of the LGU who appeared in the master list of paid beneficiaries of the ESP be refunded.
observations.managementAction: A written explanation on the difference between the target beneficiaries and actual beneficiaries and on the lapses and deficiencies was submitted. Management instituted measures to improve profiling and validation of beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor the following documents to substantiate the grant of SRA: a. Inter-Agency Task Force (IATF) for the Management of Emerging Infectious Diseases Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace; b. Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well as the entitlement of the SRA; c. Individual approved daily time record (DTR) or daily report of attendance indicating the dates and time they physically reported for work; and d. Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs quarantined in the Municipality’s COVID-19 facility, in the homes, and in other isolation facilities covering the same period of the ECQ.
observations.managementAction: The IATF Certification had been submitted. The daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients had not been submitted. Some DTRs attached to the payrolls were incomplete. The Authority from the head of agency/office to render service had not been attached.
observations.reason: Authority from the head of agency/office to render service had not been attached. Incomplete DTRs were attached to the payrolls. The daily status/monitoring report had not been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee procure medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the SRP set by appropriate government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: The MHO already submitted a written justification to prove reasonableness for the procurement of the aforesaid items above the suggested price of the DOH. Henceforth, procure medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the SRP set by the appropriate government agencies.
observations.reason: Some items subsequently purchased still exceeded the DOH-recommended prices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned personnel or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to completely and properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Purchase orders attached to CY 2021 disbursement vouchers still lacked the relevant and required information.
observations.reason: Some items purchased still lacked the relevant and required information in the purchase orders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. make sure to reflect the correct method of procurement to be undertaken in the APP; b. always indicate in the PO the appropriate mode of procurement undertaken; and c. strictly comply with the procurement process mandated by the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and other issuances of the Government Procurement Policy Board. Moreover, submit the lacking required documents to the Office of the Auditor, and comply with the documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for future disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The proper mode of procurement had already been indicated in the APP. The mode of procurement per APP failed to comply with the procurement rules under RA No. 9184, particularly those under Negotiated Procurement that exceeded ₱100,000.00. No documents had been submitted in compliance with the prior year’s observation.
observations.reason: The mode of procurement had not been indicated in some purchase orders. The mode of procurement in the APP failed to comply with procurement rules under RA No. 9184. No documents had been submitted in compliance with the prior year’s observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Budget Officer to properly maintain the Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations. The format of these forms can be referred to in Annexes 13, 14, 15 and 16 of Volume II of the MNGAS. At the back of these forms are instructions on how it shall be accomplished.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer had already prepared the RAAO for CY 2021. The RAAO was prepared but projects had not been specified and only the account titles charged had been enumerated.
observations.reason: Some obligations exceeded their appropriations signifying that the RAAO was not properly utilized as a monitoring tool. Projects had not been specified and only the account titles charged had been enumerated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately stop from utilizing the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not properly chargeable to the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF and transfer it back to the unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund-CO to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements, and that the funds will not be made available for other disaster risk reduction and management activities. We also recommended that the LDRRMFIP be prepared in substantial adherence to the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002, thereby making this trust fund available for disbursement.
observations.managementAction: The closing of CY 2021 accounts was in accordance with the recommendation by not augmenting appropriations without any authority from the Sangguniang Bayan. The PPAs chargeable to the prior year’s unexpended LDRRMF had been incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: PPAs which are not properly chargeable thereto were still among the items found in the LDRRMFIP. Some PPAs had not been incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. immediately stop from utilizing the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not properly chargeable to the Fund; b. henceforth, the PPAs to be included in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan should conform to the PPAs enumerated in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and c. the amount of ₱517,073.34 representing a portion of the LDRRMF appropriations spent for expenses not properly chargeable thereto, should be refunded from the appropriations of the General Fund Proper.
observations.managementAction: The PPAs to be included under the CY 2021 20% DF were implemented in accordance with the development plan as required under Section 476 (4) of R.A. No. 7160. The LDRRM Fund appropriations were not augmented without a supporting appropriation ordinance.
observations.reason: PPAs which are not properly chargeable thereto were still among the items found in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from increasing any appropriations without any legal basis and authority to prevent illegal expenditures. Moreover, we recommended that Management ensure that any disbursement out of the unexpended prior year balance of the LDRRM Fund be supported with a revised AIP, duly approved by the local sanggunian and the Municipal Mayor.
observations.managementAction: Management had not augmented appropriations without any authority from the Sangguniang Bayan. The Municipal Accountant had already adjusted the beginning balances of budgets in accordance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, return the proceeds transferred to the Special Trust Fund to the unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund-CO to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements and that the funds will not be made available for other disaster risk reduction and management activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer transferred funds back as required. The return of certain funds to the Bureau of Treasury were processed.
observations.reason: Some CO proceeds were still being sorted out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the report to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer had prepared and submitted the Monthly Report for certain months and the fourth quarter of CY 2021. Some monthly reports were not submitted timely.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Officer had only submitted the required report for August and the fourth quarter of CY 2021. No monthly submission was made for other months.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer return the unexpended balances of programs/projects to their respective national source agencies in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005 so that these funds can be used for other priority programs/projects. We also recommended that Management transfer proceeds from rice and corn seeds to the unappropriated general fund in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: A portion of the unused balances was returned to the National Treasury. In CY 2021, returned funds to the Bureau of Treasury were: ₱300,000.00 and ₱85,852.15 from Senator Cynthia Villar’s PDAF and prior years’ PDAF, respectively. The Municipal Treasurer returned proceeds from rice and corn seeds totaling ₱119,262.50 to the unappropriated general fund.
observations.reason: Other funds were still being sorted out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all PHWs and other entitled personnel to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks and certifications from appropriate agencies and the documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to establish validity of the payments.
observations.managementAction: The DRRMO and Municipal Health Office provided some supporting documents. The Municipal Health Office had already submitted some relevant documents but not all required supporting documents were attached as prescribed under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: A number of supporting documents specified under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 were not attached as supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, evaluate the feasibility, and closely monitor the implementation of the different PPAs of the Municipality in accordance with the development plan as required under Section 476 (4) of R.A. No. 7160. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the number of personnel assigned in the Office of the Municipal Engineer, taking into consideration their profiles and technical qualifications. Lastly, we recommended that Management consider implementing some of the projects by contract.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO prioritized planning and implementation where possible. Some PPAs under CY 2021 20% DF were implemented and closely monitored by contract.
observations.reason: Optimal use of the 70% Mitigation Fund had not yet been achieved and some backlog projects remained. Implementation of PPAs under the 20% DF was minimal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up an already issued journal entry to reflect the correct amount of RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2018. We further recommended that henceforth, RPT/SET Receivables be recognized at the beginning of each year on the basis of the Certified List of Taxpayers and Amounts Due and Collectible for the Year prepared by the Municipal Treasurer, and that the latter continually update the list on the basis of the assessment roll of newly-assessed/reassessed real properties prepared by the Municipal Assessor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant took up the journal entries to adjust RPT/SET Receivable balances as of December 31, 2021.
observations.reason: There was only one of the 38 PPAs programmed under the CY 2021 20% DF implemented. (Note: some cross references in the table indicate steps still pending)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue already issued demand letters to the concerned groups or individuals who have been identified as borrowers of the recorded loans. We further recommended that Management exhaust all efforts to collect these loans and determine collectability thereof. For loans whose borrowers are not identified, we recommended that the Municipality request write-off from the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the loan receivable balance of CBRMP loans against the list of outstanding loans and effect the necessary entries in order to fairly present the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already issued demand letters to the concerned borrowers. Some demand letters were issued. The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.
observations.reason: No collections were made for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant return or remit the balances of the programs/projects to their respective national source agencies. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant return the unutilized PDAF to the National Treasury, as ordered by the Supreme Court, and that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary and appropriate adjustments, where applicable, so that the Due to NGAs account will be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management already returned ₱385,852.15 to the National Treasury broken down as ₱300,000.00 and ₱85,852.15 from Senator Cynthia Villar’s PDAF and prior years’ PDAF, respectively, per Check No. 74182240 dated April 29, 2021. The Municipal Accountant prepared related adjustments where necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the DRRMO prioritize planning, implementation and monitoring of PPAs geared towards disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness aligned with the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and regularly monitor its implementation to ensure the municipal government’s resilience to disaster.
observations.managementAction: The DRRMO prioritized planning and implemented some of the PPAs identified. Various PPAs addressing disaster prevention and mitigation were implemented.
observations.reason: Optimal use of the 70% Mitigation Fund had not yet been achieved. Some PPAs identified had not been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Office, in coordination with the various officials in the community and its component barangays, prioritize compliance with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness as required under R.A. 10121 and other relevant laws, rules and regulations using the NDPP Minimum Standards manual published by the Local Government Academy in order to be better prepared to handle and cope with natural calamities which may occur.
observations.managementAction: The DRRM Officer had already coordinated with the various barangay officials regarding compliance with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness. Staff had been designated for logistics and to respond to emergencies 24/7.
observations.reason: Some criteria in the minimum standards had not yet been complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to establish and maintain a GAD database in order to provide the data necessary to properly plan GAD projects and activities. We also recommended that the municipal mayor create a Monitoring and Evaluation System in order that GAD projects and activities are properly planned and implemented and results assessed to ensure its success.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO gathered raw data necessary in the GAD database and budgeted ₱1,894,204.32 for CY 2020 for the Community-Based Management Information System (CBMIS). The municipality was waiting for the PSA to conduct a Community-Based Management Information System which is an important component in the establishment of the GAD database.
observations.reason: The CY 2021 GAD Accomplishment Report was not submitted. The establishment of the GAD database was still pending pending PSA action and other processes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Program of Work be properly prepared based on detailed engineering measurements, duly reviewed by the Municipal Engineer before the same is approved in order to avoid the purchase of excess materials and loss of government funds, and that in cases where excesses cannot be avoided, the Municipal Engineer see to it that the excess materials are reprogrammed for utilization in other projects of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit an explanation for the excess quantities of materials programmed, the neglect to properly account and store the excess and to reprogram these to other projects of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted explanations and measures to avoid recurrence in the future.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer had not formally submitted a detailed explanation for the excess quantities of materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management give priority to the implementation of GAD projects, especially those which were not implemented in CY 2016 in order that the GAD needs of women and the objectives of the program will be achieved.
observations.managementAction: Some GAD projects were implemented. Based on the RAAO, only a minimal amount was expended from the current year’s budget.
observations.reason: The CY 2021 GAD Accomplishment Report was not submitted. Implementation of GAD projects had not been sufficiently prioritized for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake measures to ensure that the amount paid by the LGU to the bank is recovered through means acceptable to both parties, which should require an amendment to the MOA.
observations.managementAction: According to the 2008 MPDO, the facility is currently used by the Municipality and a ₱50,000.00 monthly payment under the moratorium entered into by the Municipality and the Water District in CY 2016 was being recovered. A MOA amendment was recommended.
observations.reason: A ₱41,068.01 balance remained as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management should come up with a solution on how to utilize the project the soonest possible time to prevent damage to the property that might result from non-utilization and exposure to both natural and man-made elements.
observations.managementAction: According to the 2008 MPDO, the facility is currently used by the Municipal Agriculture Office as a Bagsakan Center/ store-outlet and showroom of farm and agri-products from the different barangays of the Municipality. Management advised that application for titling of the real properties had not yet started because of numerous documents required in the process.
observations.reason: The application for titling of the real properties had not yet started due to numerous required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require all PHWs and other entitled personnel to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks, certifications from appropriate agencies and the documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to establish validity of payments.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented
observations.reason: Perusal of the disbursement vouchers for hazard pay revealed that none of the documents specified under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 were attached as supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that only programs, projects and activities aligned with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 or listed therein are charged to the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented
observations.reason: There were still inappropriate charges to the 20% Development Fund as discussed in Finding No. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that only programs, projects and activities aligned with RA No. 10121 and DILG MC No. 2012-73 or listed therein are charged to the LDRRM fund and that the procurement of an ambulance shall be appropriately charged to the 20% Development Fund, under social services.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented
observations.reason: There were still inappropriate charges to the LDRRM Fund as discussed in Finding No. 15, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the Municipality charge to the General Fund the regular maintenance and other operating expenses of the LDRRMO and utilize the LDRRMF only for those expenses which are related to disaster preparedness and mitigation activities in order that the latter fund will be optimally utilized for the Municipality’s preparation for disasters which may possibly occur.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented
observations.reason: Other operating expenses of the LDRRMO were still charged against the LDRRM Fund as discussed in Finding No. 15, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, return or remit the balances of the programs/projects to their respective source national agencies. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the provincial liga member acknowledge its payment with government accountable form No. 51 in order that the funds may be subject to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations. We also recommended that the provincial liga adhere to DILG MC 2011-59.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of the 20% Development Fund Budget, a Program of Work be required before a project can be included therein in order that the implementation thereof will not be delayed.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipality found it impractical to prepare the Programs of Work in the budgeting process due to price escalations. The Municipality had other backlog projects that they prioritized.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the municipal government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the Municipality of Ayungon can be issued to establish the Municipality's ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: AAR Not Implemented
observations.reason: The application for titling of real properties was not yet started because of the numerous documents needed in the process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. Management direct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct
of the physical count of inventories and properties and that RPCI and
RPCPPE be submitted semestrally and annually, respectively;
b. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be required
to maintain and subsequently update the Ledger Cards and Stock and
Property Cards in order that the reconciliation of the physical count
against book balances can be facilitated; and
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the
accounting and property records and make the necessary adjustments
so that the accounts can be fairly presented in the financial statements.
In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the
provisions of Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, specifically
charging of inventory to their appropriate expense accounts upon issuance
thereof.

We recommended that Management desist from charging ineligible
expenditures from BGCM and refund the BGCM for the amount expended to be
sourced out from the General Fund Proper.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete supporting
documents in order to ascertain the propriety of the disbursement
made;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that
payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with
completely filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the
propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption;
c. Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to completely fill out the necessary
data or information on the driver’s trip ticket; and
d. Ensure that meals, snacks, catering services are duly supported with
activity design, menu, and attendance of barangay officials and
volunteers involved in the activity.

We recommended that Management provide a written explanation regarding the
payment of the purchase of rice through cash advances, and refrain from granting of
cash advance for payment of regular expenditures of the Municipality in the future.
Moreover, the rules and regulations on the granting and utilization of cash advance be
strictly followed in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10,
1997.

We recommended that Management
a. verify that projects to be funded by the 20% DF, and subsequent
disbursements made thereof conform to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1
dated February 22, 2017 in order that the desired socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes geared mainly
towards the creation of jobs, promotion of economic growth and
eradication of poverty, as envisioned by the national government, may
be achieved;
b. immediately stop from utilizing the 20% DF for projects, programs and
activities which are not properly chargeable thereto;
c. the amount representing portion of the 20% DF spent for expenses
which are not properly chargeable thereto, should be refunded from the
appropriations of the General Fund Proper.
d. advise the MPDC Officer to prepare a revised AIP for the 20% DF for
the realignment of those unobligated appropriations which were not
properly chargeable thereto.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management direct the Municipal
Accountant to furnish the Auditor copies of the contract of the foregoing projects
together with the supporting documents for review of the latter. Moreover,
Management is required to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No.
2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Management
a. render proper accounting of the difference noted from the purchased
and distributed food items;
b. install sound internal control mechanism in the repacking of goods to
monitor the release of items for repacking and account the packed
relief items to ensure that these are safeguarded from possible theft,
loss and wastage;
c. require the MDRRMO or property officer to prepare reports on the
inventory and monitoring of all procured and donated supplies,
materials, equipment, and relief goods in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and
d. direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies,
medicines, and relief goods for distribution in accordance with the
aforementioned COA Circular.

We recommended that (a) the MSWDO submit a written explanation on
the difference between the number of the target beneficiaries (8,047) and the
actual beneficiaries found on the master list (8,031) and on the lapses and
deficiencies enumerated in this observation; (b) require Management to establish
a system in the distribution of SAP and similar cash assistance programs wherein
the guidelines issued by the DSWD are followed and that proper validation and
appropriate profiling of beneficiaries are performed before disbursement; and (c)
the amount received by the 13 job order personnel of the LGU who appeared in
the master list of paid beneficiaries of the ESP be refunded.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer
to submit to the Auditor the following documents to substantiate the grant of SRA:
a) Inter-Agency Task Force (IATF) for the Management of Emerging
Infectious Diseases Resolution of the Risk Certification in the
workplace;
b) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the
period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well
as the entitlement of the SRA;
c) Individual approved daily time record (DTR) or daily report of
attendance indicating the dates and time they physically reported for
work; and
d) Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs
quarantined in the Municipality’s COVID-19 facility, in the homes, and
in other isolation facilities covering the same period of the ECQ.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards
Committee to submit justification to prove reasonableness for the procurement of
the aforecited items above the suggested price of the DOH. Henceforth, procure
medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the SRP set by appropriate
government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the
government.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned
personnel or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to
completely and properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO
pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that Management require the Chairperson and
members of the Bids and Awards Committee to:
a. make sure to reflect the correct method of procurement to be
undertaken in the APP;
b. always indicate in the PO the appropriate mode of procurement
undertaken; and
c. strictly comply with the procurement process mandated by the IRR of
R.A. No. 9184 and other issuances of the Government Procurement
Policy Board.

Moreover, we recommended that Management submit the lacking
required documents to the Office of the Auditor, and comply with the
documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for future
disbursements.

We recommended and Management agreed to advise the Municipal
Budget Officer to properly maintain the Registries of Appropriations, Allotments
and Obligations. The format of these forms can be referred to in Annexes 13, 14,
15 and 16 of Volume II of the MNGAS. At the back of these forms are instructions
on how it shall be accomplished.

We recommended that Management instruct the LDRRM Officer to
include PPAs chargeable against the prior years unexpended LDRRMF in the
annual LDRRMFIP of the current year pursuant to NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC
No. 2013-1 and the same be prepared in substantial adherence to the format
prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002, thereby make this trust
fund available for disbursement.

We recommended that Management
a. immediately stop from utilizing the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not
properly chargeable to the Fund;
b. henceforth, the PPAs to be included in the annual LDRRMF
Investment Plan should conform to the PPAs enumerated in
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and
c. the amount of ₱517,073.34 representing a portion of the LDRRMF
appropriations spent for expenses not properly chargeable thereto,
should be refunded from the appropriations of the GF Proper.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from increasing
any appropriations without any legal basis and authority to prevent illegal
expenditures. Moreover, we recommended that Management ensure that any
disbursement out of the unexpended prior year balance of the LDRRM Fund be
supported with a revised AIP, duly approved by the local sanggunian and the
Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Treasurer to identify the amount of the LDRRM Fund related to CO which
was transferred to the Special Trust Fund, and transfer it back the to the
unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund – CO to the General Fund to ensure
fair presentation of the financial statements, and that the funds will not be made
available for other disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer prepare the Monthly Report
on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting
records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the report
to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that: a. the Municipal Agriculturist, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, conduct an inventory of breeding stocks and plants and trees as of the end of each year and thereafter render a report including the fair market value of these items to the Municipal Accountant, for proper recording in the books of the LGU; b. the Municipal Agriculturist henceforth keep a systematic record of the Municipality’s breeding stocks and trees, reporting births, deaths and disposal, and in case of death or disposal, facilitate request for relief of accountability in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 to appropriately account and drop the amount from the books; and c. the Municipal Accountant disclose the composition of the Biological Assets account in the Notes to the Financial Statements in accordance with PPSAS 27 to provide relevant information that may affect the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR An inventory of breeding stocks and plants and trees was conducted last June 2019 and an Inventory Report was submitted and used as basis of recording these biological assets in the books of the LGU. The Municipal Agriculturist maintained a logbook and monthly meetings are conducted with the barangay livestock aides for updates and proper recordkeeping. The Municipal Accountant disclosed the composition of the Biological Assets account in the Notes to the Financial Statements, particularly, Note 9 – Biological Assets.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, return the unexpended balances of programs/projects to their respective source national agencies in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005 so that these funds can be used for other priority programs/projects. We also recommended that Management transfer the proceeds from rice and corn seeds totaling to ₱119,262.50 to the unappropriated general fund in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: AAR The ₱195,623.98 unused balance was made as funds available on August 2020 and shall form part of the beginning balances for Budget Year 2021.
observations.reason: Awaiting further instructions from the DBM on how to facilitate the return of the other unused funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, evaluate the feasibility, and closely monitor the implementation of, the different PPAs of the Municipality in accordance with the approved development plan as required under Section 476 (4) of R.A. No. 7160. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the number of personnel assigned in the Office of the Municipal Engineer, taking into consideration their profiles and technical qualifications. Lastly, we recommended that Management consider implementing some of the projects by contract.
observations.managementAction: AAR Planning was given a priority by the Municipal DRRMO and various PPAs which address the 70% Mitigation and mitigation were identified. 28 out of 48 or 58.33% projects were completed for CY 2020. 40 out of 48 or 83.33% of projects were implemented for CY 2020 by contract.
observations.reason: The number of personnel assigned in the Office of the Municipal Engineer was not evaluated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the RPT/SET Receivables be recognized at the beginning of each year on the basis of the Certified List of Taxpayers and Amounts Due and Collectible for the Year prepared by the Municipal Treasurer, and that the latter continually update the list on the basis of the assessment roll of newly-assessed/reassessed properties prepared by the Municipal Assessor. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the concerned groups or individuals who have been identified as borrowers of the recorded loans. We further recommended that Management exhaust all efforts to collect these loans and to determine collectability thereof. For loans whose borrowers are not identified, we recommended that the Municipality request for write-off from the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the loan receivable balance of CBRMP loans against the list of outstanding loans and effect the necessary entries in order to fairly present the account in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that:

a) the Municipal Agricultural Officer, in coordination with the Municipal
Treasurer, conduct an inventory of breeding stocks and plants and
trees as of the end of each year and thereafter render a report including
the fair market value of these items to the Municipal Accountant, for
proper recording in the books of the LGU;

b) the Municipal Agriculturist henceforth keep a systematic record of the
Municipality’s breeding stocks and trees, reporting births, deaths and
disposal, and in case of death or disposal, facilitate request for relief of

October 20, 1992 to appropriately drop the amount from the books;
and

c) the Municipal Accountant disclose the composition of the Biological
Assets account in the Notes to the Financial Statements in accordance
with PPSAS 27 to provide relevant information that may affect the
fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with the Municipal Accountant, return the unexpended balances of
programs/projects to their respective source national agencies in accordance with
COA Circular 2016-005 so that these funds can be used for other priority
programs/projects. We also recommended and management agreed to transfer
the proceeds from rice and corn seeds totaling to ₱119,262.50 to the
unappropriated general fund in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and
2016-005.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require all PHWs and
other entitled personnel to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of
actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks, certifications
from appropriate agencies and the documents required under Section 5.8 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to establish validity of

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that disbursements of funds are covered by appropriations to avoid illegal expenditures. We further recommended that Obligation Requests be properly filled-up in order that charges to appropriations can be controlled and the propriety reviewed prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: The municipality ensured that disbursements were covered with appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality desist from using government funds for sound system for beauty pageant.
observations.managementAction: The municipality stopped spending for beauty pageant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth the procurement of diesel fuel and gasoline must be done through public bidding pursuant to the provisions RA 9184 or the Government Procurement Reform Act.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of fuel are now being done through public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to investigate on the gross violation by personnel in the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and if warranted, impose sanctions against erring personnel in accordance with these laws.
observations.managementAction: The management required all the personnel involved in the procurement of items which prefer a particular supplier to refund the amount of disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the charging of food items for meals and snacks during meetings, instead to the appropriations for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses and Representation Allowances as provided under Section 343 of the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: The municipality stopped charging meals and snacks during meetings.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our previous recommendations that: a. Management require the conduct of the physical inventory count of the properties and submission of the corresponding report to the audit team. b. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the accounting and property records and make the necessary adjustments so that the accounts can be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Initially inventory count was conducted. However, it was not completed.
observations.reason: The personnel conducting the count did not prioritize the completion of the count.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management undertake measures to ensure that the amount paid by the LGU to the bank is recovered through means acceptable to both parties, which should require an amendment to the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality collected ₱600,000.00 in CY 2017. ₱50,000.00 monthly payment under the moratorium entered into by the municipality and the Water District in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Prepare and submit to the Auditor the Income Statement and Balance Sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of January 31, 2013. b. Furnish the auditor a copy of Article III of the required monitoring and evaluation reports, the status of the Project Implementation including the report on the marketing strategies and management system aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the purpose of the BB Project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant took over the bookkeeping of the Barangay Bagsakan Project and have prepared the financial statements. However, it was not submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management come up with a solution on how to utilize the project the soonest possible time to prevent damage to the property that might result due to long non-utilization and exposure to both natural and man-made elements. Management is advised to conduct careful and proper study and evaluation of any project proposal before its prosecution.
observations.managementAction: The building is now being utilized as office of the Ayungon Water District.
observations.reason: Feedmill is still not operational because some of the parts were not available due to fortuitous event that hit the supplier.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to maintain their respective property records for each real property and to prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the municipal government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the Municipality of Ayungon can be issued to establish the municipality’s ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the bookkeeping of the Barangay Bagsakan Project as part of corrective action; process was started.
observations.reason: The application for titling of real properties was not yet started because of the numerous documents needed in the process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the LDRRMO to observe the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 in the use of the LDRRMF to ensure proper utilization of the fund and to provide appropriation from the general fund to cover the cost of vehicles purchased.
observations.managementAction: There were still some disbursements under the LDRRMF which were not aligned to the Circular.
observations.reason: There were still some disbursements under the LDRRMF which were not aligned to the Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the charging of food items for drivers because to them, it is economical.
observations.managementAction: The municipality continued to purchase food items for drivers because to them, it is economical.
observations.reason: Municipality continued to purchase food items for drivers because to them, it is economical.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our previous recommendations that: a. Management require the conduct of the physical inventory count of the properties and submission of the corresponding report to the audit team. b. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the accounting and property records and make the necessary adjustments so that the accounts can be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2016 , the balance of the municipality’s movable property, plant and equipment amounted to ₱35,687,298.27.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRMO to observe the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 in the use of the LDRRMF so that proper implementation of the fund will be carried out effectively and efficiently.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Finding No. 4 of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Prepare and submit to the Auditor the Income Statement and Balance Sheet on the operation of the Barangay Bagsakan Project; b. Furnish the auditor a copy of the required monitoring and evaluation reports, the status of the Project Implementation including the report on the marketing strategies and management system which aimed to ensure project sustainability measures and the achievement of the objectives of the BB Project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal MPDC explained that the Barangay Bagsakan Project as of January 31, 2013 had the Income Statement and Balance Sheet prepared. The MPDC submitted documentation.
