observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to maintain respective property records. Prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the municipal government so transfer certificates of title in favor of the Municipality of Ayungon can be issued.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor was tasked to secure respective property records for each real property and to prioritize the filing of the application for titling.
observations.reason: There was still no breakdown / no transfer certificates of title issued.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to dispose obsolete and unserviceable properties and conduct appraisal of the properties and in the same token the Municipal Accountant to record the disposal in accordance with Sections 162 and 195 of COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Unserviceable properties are still not disposed because appraisal of the properties has not yet been conducted.
observations.reason: Appraisal of the properties has not yet been conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee be required to
submit to the auditor within the reglementary period of five (5) working
days from perfection, two (2) sets of the contract and all supporting
documents for the auditorial and technical review thereof pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the start-up expenses for the implementation
of the KALAHI-CIDSS-NCDDP program be funded by the General Fund
and that the counterpart funds sourced from the 20% Development Fund be
used exclusively for the actual project implementation pursuant to DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. We further recommended
that the 20% Counterpart Funds for projects be reimbursed for the amount
utilized for the administrative expenses.

We recommended that the municipality desist from providing
uniforms to volunteer firemen, volunteer dog catchers as well as job order
personnel and henceforth abide by the rules and regulations on the grant
thereof in order to avoid incurring irregular expenditures.

We recommended that the municipal officials, particularly the
LDRRMO and the Mayor, observe caution in the use of the LDRRMF and
henceforth strictly observe the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and other similar issuances in order to
ensure the proper utilization thereof. We further recommended that the
Municipal Mayor clarify the responsibilities of LGU Ayungon with respect to
the acceptance of the services of a Peace Corps volunteer and that only those
which are provided in the MOA are charged to municipal funds.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board exercise caution
in the use of the SEF and limit the utilization of the fund to the programs and
projects defined under RA 7160, DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No.
01 dated April 14, 1998 and LOI No. 1462 in order that the primary
objectives of the fund can be attained.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that
disbursements of funds are covered by appropriations to avoid illegal
expenditures. We further recommend that Obligation Requests be properly
filled up in order that charges to appropriations can be controlled and the
propriety reviewed prior to payment.

We recommended that the municipality desist from using government
funds for beauty pageants or any other forms of entertainment in accordance
with Section 343 of R. A. 7160.

We recommended that the employee in charge of the renewal of the
registration of motor vehicles ensure the completeness of requirements for
motor vehicle registration or renewal and monitor, as well, the expiration
dates thereof in order to avoid incurrence of penalties for delayed
registration.

We recommended that in the preparation of the 20% Development
Fund Budget, a Program of Work be required before a project can be
included therein in order that the implementation thereof will not be delayed.

We recommended that the use of the vehicles procured under the
LDRRMF for ordinary, day-to-day operations of the municipality be
immediately stopped in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles to times
of actual calamity in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2014-155, otherwise, the LDRRMF must be reimbursed by the General Fund
for the cost of these vehicles.

We recommended that management give priority to the
implementation of GAD projects especially those which were not
implemented in CY 2016 inorder that the needs of women and the objectives
of the program will be achieved.

We recommended that henceforth the procurement of diesel fuel and
gasoline must be done through public bidding pursuant to the provisions of
RA 9184 or the Government Procurement Reform Act.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee be required to explain in writing for not advertising subject procurement in a newspaper of general nationwide circulation. Henceforth, in a newspaper of nationwide circulation.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted an explanation that they were not able to post the procurement in the PhilGEPS and LGU website with the assumption the nationwide publication. Henceforth, they were able to post the bidding in the PhilGEPS and LGU website with the publication.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an appraisal committee to appraise the value of each asset and accordingly, make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts. Also, require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit an explanation why the bid price of the equipment was in lump amount.
observations.managementAction: The municipality identified the value of each equipment and adjusted the books accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit an explanation for the partial completion of the Ayungon Multi-Purpose Sports Complex and henceforth, in prosecuting future projects, a program of work shall be prepared based on a detailed engineering which should also be the basis in the procurement of materials through public bidding to obtain the lowest and most advantageous prices.
observations.managementAction: The municipality stopped preparing piece meal program of works for completion of works for the Ayungon Multi-Purpose Sports Complex.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management investigate on the gross violation by the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) of the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and if warranted, impose sanctions against erring personnel in accordance with these laws.
observations.managementAction: Management required all the personnel involved in the procurement of items which prefer a particular supplier to refund the amount of disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that cash advances granted for payment of salaries, wages, allowances, honoraria and similar expenses be supported with approved payrolls or list of the payees to ascertain that the amount of cash advance is equal to the net pay of the payroll.
observations.managementAction: All cash advances made by the disbursing officer were supported with list of payees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant refrain from charging prior year's expenditures to current appropriations. In isolated cases when the charging of prior year’s expenditures to current appropriations cannot be avoided, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Mayor should request for an appropriation and authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer stopped the practice of charging prior year’s charges to current appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the guidelines on the granting of Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation and to, henceforth, ensure that the disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentary requirements in order to establish the validity of the transactions made.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of burial materials were now supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to explain in writing the rental of chairs despite available chairs at the TESDA Training Center. Henceforth, exercise prudence in spending government funds and refrain from incurring unnecessary expenditures in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-003.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that during this time, many TESDA classes were undertaken and that the purchased chairs were not enough. Management submitted the list of participants of the TESDA courses offered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Mayor take immediate appropriate action on the contract for the completion of the Small Water Impounding Project at Barangay Banban which was not completed by its contractor, such as termination, takeover, award of the contracts to other contractors and forfeiture of performance bonds pursuant to Section 8.4 of Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 in order that the projects can already be utilized for their intended purposes. b. The Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned be made to explain their failure to monitor the completion of the project, the seeming lack of planning in the prosecution of the project, their lack of initiative to recommend alternative courses of action as provided under the IRR of R.A. 9184. c. The necessary liquidated damages, specifically on the unjustified delay in the implementation of the project be computed, imposed and collected. d. Henceforth, to adequately plan and supervise the implementation of infrastructure projects to ensure that these are completed as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor explained that the delay in completion was due to the weather condition and thus justifiable. The project was already 100% completed and inspected by COA TAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Require the Liga ng mga Barangay to issue official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines in acknowledging payments received by them. b. Require the Municipality Accountant to recognize in the books receivables from the league and monitor the utilization of the funds released by requiring the recipient league concerned to submit liquidation reports. The following entry was already should be made: DR 149 200,000.00 CR 878 200,000.00 In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated. c. Require the league concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-Laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recognized the receivable in the books and submitted the copy of the Constitution and By-Laws.

observations.recommendation: Require the officials concerned to submit approval for the trip from the DILG Regional Office as well as the approved LGU CapDev Agenda showing that Lakbay-Aral or study tours may be implemented in pursuance thereof. Henceforth, secure authority from the DILG Regional Director for study tours where expenses are charged to municipal funds pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007-71.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the Lakbay-Aral does not need the DILG authorization. Henceforth, secure authority where applicable.
observations.reason: Management did not submit the approved LGU CapDev.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management undertake measures to ensure that the amount paid by the LGU to the bank is recovered through means acceptable to both parties, which should require an amendment to the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant approved a moratorium for the Water District not to pay in CY 2016. However, they will resume paying the municipality at ₱50,000.00 per month in CY 2017.
observations.reason: Only partial collection was made in CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management should come up with a solution on how to utilize the project at the soonest possible time to prevent damage to the property that might result due to long non-utilization and exposure to both natural and man-made elements. Management is advised to conduct careful and proper study of any project proposal before its prosecution.
observations.managementAction: The building is now being utilized by the Municipality since it was hit by a fortuitous event that hit the location of the supplier. The feedmill is still not operational because some of the parts are not yet available.
observations.reason: Feedmill is still not operational because some of the parts are not yet available.
